PreActions.description: Actions to perform before starting the workflow sequence. Used to inject context or pre-read files.

injectContext.description: Context text to inject into the agent's prompt before workflow execution. Typically contains workflow-specific instructions or checklists.
injectContext.title: Inject Context

readFiles.description: List of file paths (relative to project root) to read before workflow execution. Used to pre-load templates, guides, or reference documents.
readFiles.title: Read Files

PreActions.title: Pre-Actions

enabled.description: Master toggle to enable or disable all workflow detection and automation.
enabled.title: Enable Workflows

showDetection.description: Whether to display the detected intent and workflow sequence to the user before execution.
showDetection.title: Show Detection

StepMeta.description: Optional runtime hints for a specific workflow step. Read by the model (per the model-driven advancement rule) to decide sub-agent vs inline execution. Overrides values in the skill's SKILL.md front matter.

contextBudget.description: Expected context consumption of this step. 'critical' = always run as sub-agent. 'high' = recommend sub-agent. 'medium' = optional sub-agent. 'low' = inline safe.
contextBudget.title: Context Budget

executionMode.description: Explicit execution mode override. 'subagent' = MUST run via Agent tool. 'inline' = safe to run as Skill tool call.
executionMode.title: Execution Mode

StepMeta.title: Step Metadata

WorkflowEntry.description: Defines a workflow selected by AI-native intent detection. Contains natural language descriptions for when to use/not use, an ordered sequence of commands, and optional pre-actions.

description.description: Detailed description of what this workflow does and when it is triggered.
description.title: Workflow Description

name.description: Human-readable display name for the workflow, shown to users during detection.
name.title: Workflow Name

parallelGroups.description: Optional declaration of parallel-phase barrier groups within `sequence`. Each group's members run concurrently as ONE all-return barrier: spawn ALL members together (one message), then advance past the group ONLY after EVERY member returns (a skipped conditional member counts as returned). This is the portable, machine-visible complement to the prose advancement rule — the sync renderer collapses a group's members into a single `[parallel ⇉ all-return barrier: ...]` token in the Codex context mirrors, so the barrier is visible to hookless tools that read only the rendered sequence. Every member MUST be a value present in this workflow's `sequence`, members SHOULD be contiguous in `sequence`, and a member MUST NOT appear in more than one group.

barrier.description: Always true — the group is an all-return barrier: the workflow advances past the group only after every member has returned. Declared explicitly so the structure self-documents the barrier semantics.
barrier.title: All-Return Barrier

conditionalMembers.description: Subset of `members` that are conditionally included (spawned only when their trigger is present). A skipped conditional member still counts as 'returned' for the barrier. Rendered with a `*` suffix in the mirror barrier token. Each value MUST also appear in `members`.
conditionalMembers.title: Conditional Members

id.description: Stable identifier for this parallel group (e.g. 'reviewers').
id.title: Group Id

members.description: Command keys that form the barrier. Each MUST exist in this workflow's `sequence`. Order is not significant for execution — all members are spawned together — but determines left-to-right order inside the rendered barrier token.
members.title: Group Members

parallelGroups.title: Parallel Phase Groups

sequence.description: Ordered list of step-skill names to execute. Each step resolves to the slash command /<step>. Steps run in sequence from first to last.
sequence.title: Command Sequence

stepMeta.description: Optional per-step metadata overrides keyed by command key (must match a value in sequence). Overrides execution-mode or context-budget from the skill's SKILL.md front matter. The model reads this when advancing workflow steps.
stepMeta.title: Step Metadata Overrides

whenToUse.description: Natural language description of when the AI should select this workflow. The AI model reads this to decide which workflow matches the user's intent.
examples: User reports a bug, error, crash, broken functionality, or asks to fix/debug/troubleshoot an issue.
User wants to implement, add, create, or build a new feature or functionality.
whenToUse.title: When To Use

WorkflowEntry.title: Workflow Definition

description: Schema for Claude Code workflow automation configuration. Defines AI-native intent detection and workflow sequences for AI agent orchestration.

$schema.description: Path or URL to the JSON Schema file for IDE validation and autocompletion.
$schema.title: Schema Reference

description.description: Human-readable description of this workflow configuration file.
description.title: Configuration Description

version.description: Semantic version of the workflow configuration (e.g., 2.0.0).
version.title: Configuration Version

workflows.description: Named workflow definitions selected by AI-native intent detection. Each workflow has a natural language whenToUse description that the AI reads to determine which workflow matches the user's intent.
workflows.title: Workflow Definitions

title: Workflow Configuration
